results.tldr: KANVAS is introduced—a framework based on Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks, which utilize B-spline-based, compact, and interpretable neural units—to distinguish between traditional AI roles and emerging GenAI-related positions, and indicates that GenAI positions prioritize competencies such as prompt engineering and LLM integration, whereas traditional roles emphasize statistical modeling and legacy toolkits.

results.title: Labor Market Polarization and International Macroeconomic Dynamics
Beyond the Great Recession: Labor Market Polarization and Ongoing Fertility Decline in the United States
results.abstract: In the years since the Great Recession, social scientists have anticipated that economic recovery in the U.S., characterized by gains in employment and median household income, would augur a reversal of declining fertility trends. However, the expected post-recession rebound in fertility rates has yet to materialize. In this study, I propose an economic explanation for why fertility rates have continued to decline regardless of improvements in conventional economic indicators. I argue that on

results.title: Explainable Machine Learning Financial Econometrics for Digital Inclusive Finance Impact on Rural Labor Market
results.abstract: <jats:p>The research examines how digital inclusive finance reshapes the rural labor market using an auditable index system and an interpretable learning pipeline. We construct a four-pillar framework for the rural labor market covering labor behavior, labor structure, security and fairness, and sustainability, and compute county-level scores with an Attribute Hierarchy Model plus Fuzzy Comprehensive Evaluation (AHM–FCE). Using data for 58 counties in Jiangsu from 2014 to 2023, we estimate nonli
results.tldr: A measurement-based bridge from digital inclusive finance to rural labor markets is provided by aligning access, usage, and service quality with the four pillars of the rural labor market index, which yields testable county level predictions on participation, job quality, equity, and persistence of gains.

results.title: AI-Enabled Automation, Labor Market Vulnerabilities, and Structural Transformation within an Applied Informatics Framework for Economic Analysis
results.venue: Annals of Dunarea de Jos University of Galati. Fascicle I. Economics and Applied Informatics
results.tldr: A critical analysis of the main vulnerabilities generated by AI-based automation, with a focus on the automation of repetitive tasks, the reconfiguration of occupations, and the intensification of gaps between existing and emerging skills supports the need for adaptive public policies that combine investments in education and continuous training with governance standards and occupational security.
results.abstract: A critical analysis of the main vulnerabilities generated by AI-based automation, with a focus on the automation of repetitive tasks, the reconfiguration of occupations, and the intensification of gaps between existing and emerging skills supports the need for adaptive public policies that combine investments in education and continuous training with governance standards and occupational security.

Measuring non-workers’ labor market attachment with machine learning
results.abstract: <jats:p>Studying the labor market attachment (LMA) for the non-working population is crucial for several economic outcomes, such as real wages or long-term non-employment. Official statistics rely on self-reported variables and rule-based procedures to assign the labor market status of an individual. However, this classification does not take into account other individual-level characteristics, like variables related to reservation wages or the amount and type of job offers received, implying th
results.tldr: This paper proposes a novel methodology to measure non-workers’ LMA, using the Spanish Labor Force Survey to define two groups (attached vs. non-attached), and estimates a probability distribution for each individual of belonging to such groups.

The Income Channel to Labor-Market Polarization
results.abstract: <jats:p>We study labor-market polarization by developing a framework with heterogeneous consumers, non-homothetic preferences, and endogenous labor supply a la Roy (1951). Because income-elastic sectors are intensive in high- and low-skilled occupations, shocks that raise either the average or the dispersion of income across households shift relative labor demand toward these occupations, generating an income channel to polarization. We quantify this channel by comparing the effect of shocks tha

results.title: Agentic AI and Labor Market Polarization: Who Gains, Who Loses, and How Fast?
results.abstract: <jats:p>The rapid advancement of agentic artificial intelligence (AI) systems capable of autonomous reasoning, decision-making, and multi-step task execution marks a transformative shift in the global labor landscape. Unlike previous automation waves, agentic AI can independently coordinate workflows, integrate with enterprise tools, and generate complex outputs, expanding its impact beyond routine tasks to mid-level cognitive occupations. This research investigates how agentic AI accelerates la

AI Innovation and Labor Market Polarization: Evidence from European Regions
results.abstract: <jats:p>We study whether AI innovation changes labor-market polarization by weakening the relative position of high-skilled workers. We develop a CES production framework showing how AI affects the wage-cost ratio between high- and low-skilled workers through wage and employment margins. We test the model’s predictions using European regional labor-market data and find that AI reduces the relative wage-cost ratio by nearly 7 percent on average, with effects broadly split between wage and employm

<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
               <jats:p>The paper builds an argument that international trade can be an explanation behind polarization of employment in the labor market observed in developed countries such as U.K. and U.S. It considers a small open economy, having production sectors which use three types of labor: high-skill, middle-skill and low-skill. results.abstract: <jats:p>This paper shows that labor market polarization—the shift in employment from middle-skill jobs toward high and low skill jobs—sharpens incentives to take on student debt to pursue higher education. We find that consumers experiencing deeper labor market polarization incur higher student debt balances through greater participation rates in this credit market. results.title: Modeling and Forecasting the Dynamic Impact of Artificial Intelligence on the US Labor Market: Heterogeneity Analysis Based on Machine Learning and Mixed Effects Models (2018-2024)
results.tldr: This study systematically evaluates the dynamic impact of artificial intelligence on the US labor market from 2018 to 2024, with a focus on industry-level heterogeneity, and integrates random forests, Generalized Additive Models, and Mixed-effects frameworks to quantify nonlinear relationships and sector-specific sensitivities.
results.abstract: This study systematically evaluates the dynamic impact of artificial intelligence on the US labor market from 2018 to 2024, with a focus on industry-level heterogeneity, and integrates random forests, Generalized Additive Models, and Mixed-effects frameworks to quantify nonlinear relationships and sector-specific sensitivities.

results.title: Evaluating Program Sequences with Double Machine Learning: An Application to Labor Market Policies
results.tldr: DML-based analysis of dynamic policies proves to be a useful approach within the program evaluation toolkit, and recently developed methods for estimating effects across multiple treatments and time periods are explored.
results.abstract: DML-based analysis of dynamic policies proves to be a useful approach within the program evaluation toolkit, and recently developed methods for estimating effects across multiple treatments and time periods are explored.

results.title: Indicator for the Regional Labor Market Using Machine Learning Techniques: Application to Colombian Cities
results.tldr: A methodology to estimate a labor market indicator that combines economic, social, inequality, and expectation variables is proposed that captures the traditional evolution of the employment and unemployment rates and incorporates information on gender, age, informality, productive sectors, and Google Trends data.
results.abstract: A methodology to estimate a labor market indicator that combines economic, social, inequality, and expectation variables is proposed that captures the traditional evolution of the employment and unemployment rates and incorporates information on gender, age, informality, productive sectors, and Google Trends data.

results.title: Informal Labor Market in Pakistan: Evaluating the Effectiveness of Government Relief Programs using Econometrics and Machine Learning Approach
Application of Machine Learning Algorithms for Reducing Information Asymmetry and Forecasting Social Changes in the Labor Market: Challenges and Opportunities
results.tldr: The issues of information asymmetry stemming from disparities in data access between labor market participants, as well as variations in the quality of information obtained, complicating the hiring and job search processes are addressed.
results.abstract: The issues of information asymmetry stemming from disparities in data access between labor market participants, as well as variations in the quality of information obtained, complicating the hiring and job search processes are addressed.

results.title: Machine Learning or Human Education? Thoughts and Data on Artificial Intelligence and the Labor Market: Economic Sociological Aspects of Career Orientation
results.tldr: The article reviews – from the demand side of the labor market – the skills and competences that are expected to increase and to which career guidance practitioners should pay particular attention in the coming years.
results.abstract: The article reviews – from the demand side of the labor market – the skills and competences that are expected to increase and to which career guidance practitioners should pay particular attention in the coming years.

results.title: Machine Learning in Forecasting Labor Market: A Systematic Literature Review
results.tldr: Although a wide range of ML models and data sources are employed to forecast future skills, automation risks, and labor competition, most studies fail to adequately address critical concerns such as data inconsistency, model explainability, and sequential stability of prediction over time.
results.abstract: Although a wide range of ML models and data sources are employed to forecast future skills, automation risks, and labor competition, most studies fail to adequately address critical concerns such as data inconsistency, model explainability, and sequential stability of prediction over time.

results.title: Bridging the skills gap: A machine learning approach to align business education with labor market needs in the UAE
results.tldr: This study employs neural topic modeling to cluster data from the labor market and HEIs and reveals that although business graduates remain in demand for traditional roles, there is a growing need for graduates who are equipped with modern skills.
results.abstract: This study employs neural topic modeling to cluster data from the labor market and HEIs and reveals that although business graduates remain in demand for traditional roles, there is a growing need for graduates who are equipped with modern skills.

results.title: Development of a Machine Learning Model for Forecasting the Labor Market of the Kyrgyz Republic
results.venue: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer, Data Sciences and Applications (ACDSA)

results.title: The Impact of Machine Learning on Labor Markets in EU Countries in the Context of Accelerating the Implementation of Artificial Intelligence
results.tldr: The article focused on the development of the implementation of artificial intelligence and machine learning, on changes in the labor market and the transformations of jobs, on changes in the labor market and the transformations of jobs.
results.abstract: The article focused on the development of the implementation of artificial intelligence and machine learning, on changes in the labor market and the transformations of jobs, on changes in the labor market and the transformations of jobs.

results.title: NBER WORKING PAPER SERIES SEEING BEYOND THE TREES: USING MACHINE LEARNING TO ESTIMATE THE IMPACT OF MINIMUM WAGES ON LABOR MARKET OUTCOMES
Economic polarization and the digital transformation of the labor market: the role of artificial intelligence
results.tldr: It was determined that, to ensure Ukraine’s sustainable economic development, the education system and labor market must reorient toward training specialists capable of creating and maintaining modern technologies, as well as developing sectors where the human component remains critical — education, healthcare, and creative industries.
results.abstract: It was determined that, to ensure Ukraine’s sustainable economic development, the education system and labor market must reorient toward training specialists capable of creating and maintaining modern technologies, as well as developing sectors where the human component remains critical — education, healthcare, and creative industries.

results.title: Research on Human-Machine Collaboration Efficiency in the Digital Transformation of the Labor Market
results.venue: Proceedings of the 2nd Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area International Conference on Digital Economy and Artificial Intelligence
results.tldr: A total of 158 KCP (KEY CONTROL Marks) were created to validate the effectiveness of the strategies, which provided actionable advice and implementation guidance for firms/adversational enterprises in the process of digitizing labor markets.
results.abstract: A total of 158 KCP (KEY CONTROL Marks) were created to validate the effectiveness of the strategies, which provided actionable advice and implementation guidance for firms/adversational enterprises in the process of digitizing labor markets.

results.title: AI-Driven Major Recommendations for Cloud-Based E-Learning: A Yemen Focused Solution Using Random Forest and Labor Market Data
results.tldr: The results show the opportunity of AI-based advising systems to improve student decision-making, lower dropout rates, and align academic choices with labor market needs.
results.abstract: The results show the opportunity of AI-based advising systems to improve student decision-making, lower dropout rates, and align academic choices with labor market needs.
